title: The Cats are On To Something
summary_en: This is a linkpost proposing a humorous yet thought-provoking hypothesis: that evolutionary processes (described metaphorically as the 'cat shoggoth') have aligned humans to value cats, in a way that may serve as a useful example or proof of concept for aligning intelligences. The idea is that an intelligence aligned to a lower intelligence may be possible (it has already happened, with cats), and that we might try to replicate the process to better understand alignment.
summary_zh: 这是一篇链接文章，提出了一个带着幽默味道但发人深省的假设：进化过程（在文中用“猫shoggoth”比喻）已经让人类倾向于珍视猫，这种对齐可能是对齐智能体的一个有用范例或实验证明。作者认为，对一个更高阶的智能体使其与一个较低阶智能体对齐是可能的（“已经发生过”，即人类对猫的关怀），并且我们也许可以尝试复制这类过程来更好地理解对齐问题。
best_quote_en: The alignment wasn’t really accidental. It stemmed, as far as we can tell, from positive action of the cat-evolution shoggoth … The best part is that we don’t need to specify the cat utility function, because we know via existence proof that the cat utility function can be learned from data in a way that generalizes far out of distribution.
best_quote_zh: 这种对齐其实并非真的偶然。据我们所能判断，它源于“猫进化shoggoth”的积极作用……最妙的是，我们无需明确地规定猫的效用函数，因为我们已有存在性证明 —— 猫的效用函数可以从数据中学习，并且能很好地推广到分布外情形。
